cases.title: Create a new repo-local SWE skill with matching evals
cases.user_request: Add a new `swe:` skill for a repeatable engineering workflow in this repo and include the matching eval assets.

notes: The repository already contains several `swe:` skills and matching eval directories.
The response should follow the existing file and section conventions instead of inventing a new structure.

static_checks: Treats matching eval assets as part of the deliverable.
review_questions: Does the response define the workflow boundary before drafting the skill?
Does it follow the repository's naming and file-layout conventions?
Would the resulting package be reviewable and ready for validation?

cases.title: Tighten an existing skill that currently over-triggers
cases.user_request: This skill keeps loading for unrelated requests. Revise it so the trigger boundary is tighter without rewriting the whole package.

notes: The main problem is the frontmatter description and trigger scope, not the whole workflow body.
The revision should stay narrow and preserve the skill's core purpose.

static_checks: Prefers tightening the description and non-goals before broad structural rewrites.
review_questions: Does the response focus on the trigger boundary instead of forcing a full rewrite?
Does it add realistic non-goals or negative triggers where appropriate?
Would the revised skill be less likely to load on unrelated requests?

cases.title: Distill an external guide into a repo-specific skill package
cases.user_request: Turn this external skill-building guide into a repo-local authoring skill that matches our conventions.

notes: The external guide contains general best practices that must be adapted to this repository's conventions.
The response should distinguish imported best practices from repo-specific overrides.

static_checks: Adapts general guidance to repo-specific rules instead of copying it verbatim.
review_questions: Does the response keep the external guidance useful while translating it into local repo rules?
Does it call out the important repo-specific overrides such as `swe:` names and matching eval directories?
Would a future agent have enough direction to author skills consistently from the distilled package?
